name: Ben Webster
abstract: Benjamin Francis Webster (March 27, 1909 – September 20, 1973) was an American jazz tenor saxophonist. He performed in the United States and Europe and made many recordings with Duke Ellington, Billie Holiday, Johnny Hodges, and others.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Jazz at the Philharmonic
Midnight Performance: Jazz at the Philharmonic
Duke Ellington and His Orchestra with Ella Fitzgerald
Bel Canto Foundation Jazz Concert to Benefit Musicians

about_quotes.speaker: Gary Smulyan
about_quotes.evidence: Ben Webster, Coleman Hawkins, Georgie Auld, they all play the tenor saxophone but they all have — Al Cohn, Zoot Sims — I could go on all day — Tina Brooks, Hank Mobley. You know they play one note and you know who they are immediately.

about_quotes.speaker: John Bunch
about_quotes.evidence: I’m thinking of Scott Hamilton. You know when Scott Hamilton first started I started playing with him when he was real young he came to New York. He was influenced very strongly by Ben Webster and I guess Lester Young.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Bunch interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: So him and I went out to Stan[?] Shores, a little place where they play jazz. I had a quartet. I played with Ben Webster all night long.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Fred Staton
about_quotes.evidence: And of course Coleman Hawkins, Ben Webster. And fortunately I did get a chance to meet Ben.
about_quotes.doc_title: Fred Staton interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 12, 2008

about_quotes.speaker: Schuller Gunther
about_quotes.evidence: I put together one of the greatest jazz orchestras that ever was... Ben Webster played.
about_quotes.doc_title: Schuller Gunther — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Plas Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: he should hear Ben Webster or Illinois Jacquet, Lester Young, Don Byas, Gene Ammons.
about_quotes.doc_title: Plas Johnson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996
